reasoning: HBAN recently reported a Q3 earnings beat with net income up 22% and analysts maintaining average price targets well above the current ~$15 stock price (avg target around $18.50-$19.50). This suggests modest short-term upside. For the first resolution date (Nov 16, just one week after the Nov 9 baseline), the stock benefits from near-term momentum without the drag of its Q4 dividend, since historical patterns suggest its ex-dividend date falls in the third week of November (likely Nov 19-21 in 2025). Therefore, the 1-week outlook has the best chance of remaining higher than the benchmark price. For the subsequent resolution dates (Dec 9 and Feb 7), the stock will have traded ex-dividend, effectively dropping the closing price by roughly 1% (its historical quarterly dividend yield). Accordingly, and adhering to the instruction that probabilities for distant dates must approach 0.5 to reflect compounded market noise and increasing uncertainty, I progressively adjust the estimates downward toward 50%.
batch_queries: "Huntington Bancshares" HBAN earnings financial results Q3 2025
HBAN Wall Street analyst ratings price target November 2025
regional bank stocks KRE interest rate impact November 2025
"Huntington Bancshares" OR HBAN downgrade bear case risk factors 2025
